revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mirror with linked arcs]][[ja:内行花文鏡]]


{{Short description|Han dynasty bronze mirror}}
{{more citations needed|date=June 2022}}{{Nihongo|'''Flower Mirrors'''|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|Naikoukamonkyō}} are a type of {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} that was popular in the {{ill|Eastern Han|en|Eastern Han|qid=Q1147037|lt=late Han dynasty in|1=Eastern Han}} {{ill|China (region)|en|China (region)|qid=Q29520|lt=China|1=China (region)}}. They were imported from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} to the [[Kofun period]], along with imitations made by Yayoi.

== History ==
They flourished in China in the early {{ill|Eastern Han|en|Eastern Han|qid=Q1147037|lt=Han|1=Eastern Han}} dynasty and were distributed mainly north of the {{ill|Yellow River|en|Yellow River|qid=Q7355|lt=Yellow River|1=Yellow River}} basin.{{Sfn|岡村秀典|2017|p=73-75}} It is thought that {{ill|Inscribed mirror|en|Inscribed mirror|qid=Q112835927|lt=Inscribed mirrors|1=Inscribed mirror}}, which have a series of inward arcs, were created by replacing the beveled thunder pattern, and it is characterized by a flat geometric pattern.{{Sfn|岡村秀典|2017|p=76-78}} 

== Design ==
The mirrors often have no inscriptions other than four words of good fortune in a four-leaf or four-linked arc pattern on the knob. A mirror with an ''uchigyo hana'' design in the collection of {{ill|Harvard Art Museums|en|Harvard Art Museums|qid=Q3783572|lt=Harvard University Art Museums|1=Harvard Art Museums}} was made in the 7th year of Eihei (64) at a private workshop called ''Gongsunke.'' The piece was priced at 300 sen (less than the monthly salary of a low-Ranking official), which suggests that it was a daily commodity.{{Sfn|岡村秀典|2017|p=76-78}}

They have been excavated in various places in China, as well as in the Yayoi and Kofun periods in Japan and in the Lappan tumulus in Korea, with many examples from the early and middle [[Kofun period]]. {{ill|Mikumo Nanokoji Site|lt=Mikumo Nanokoji Site|lt=Sanyumo Minamikoji Ruins|ja|三雲南小路遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyumo Minamikoji Ruins|qq=}} (31 mirrors) and the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Ruins|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (8 mirrors) were excavated from northern [[Kyushu]] in the early period.<ref>『実在した神話』[[:en:原田大六|原田大六]]著 銅鏡分布図より</ref>{{Clarification needed|reason=Text may be badly translated, see comment|date=June 2022}}

This mirror has a {{ill|Motif (visual arts)|en|Motif (visual arts)|qid=Q1229071|lt=pattern|1=Motif (visual arts)}} consisting of eight basic arcs arranged inwardly around a knob at the center of the back of the mirror. However, rare mirrors have been found with 11, 9, 6, and 5 arcs. Officially, these mirrors are called "mirrors with a continuous arc pattern", but in Japan they are called "mirrors with an uchigyo hana design" because the design is compared to [[Petal#Corolla|petals]].

Variations range from palm-sized mirrors to much larger mirrors used in rituals. Mirrors with a bead pattern placed on the knob holder, and mirrors with a straight-arc border with a pattern using straight lines and circles have been found. Some of these mirrors have a variety of images formed between the arcs and the button heads.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

== Discoveries ==
A [[:en:Large Flower Mirror|Large Flower Mirror]] with a diameter of 46.5 cm was included in a batch of artifacts excavated from the Hirabaru Square Trench Tombs in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, which was designated a {{ill|National Treasure (Japan)|en|National Treasure (Japan)|qid=Q1139795|lt=National Treasure|1=National Treasure (Japan)}} in 2006. It is the largest copper mirror excavated in Japan to date.<ref>平成18th year 6月9日文部科学省告示第74号</ref>

In 2017, a Flower Mirror in near perfect condition with little rust was excavated at the Nakajima Ruins in Iseida, {{ill|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|en|Hakata-ku, Fukuoka|qid=Q1330839|lt=Hakata-ku|1=Hakata-ku, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|qid=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka City|1=Fukuoka}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=仲島遺跡で後漢鏡が完全な姿で出土 {{!}} 発掘最前線 |url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/excavation_news/detail/30/from_sp:1 |access-date=2022-06-22 |website=福岡市の文化財 |language=ja}}</ref>
